more.title: How the Skeleton of Mutapa Times is Based on Sarah Charlesworth's Work
more.description: Conceptual artist Sarah Charlesworth's 1979 study of how newspapers covered a solar eclipse inspired the framework behind The Mutapa Times.

more.title: Empty Vaults or New Beginnings? The Real Reason RBZ Abandoned the 2030 Mono-Currency Deadline
more.description: HARARE – The Reserve Bank of Zimbabwe (RBZ) recently announced a significant policy shift, abandoning its previously set 2030 deadline for the exclusive use of a mono-currency system. While the official narrative points to a desire for “market flexibility” and a commitment to “listening to stakeholders,” an in-depth investigation by our team suggests a far [...]The post Empty Vaults or New Beginnings? The Real Reason RBZ Abandoned the 2030 Mono-Currency Deadline first appeared on My Zimbabwe News.

more.title: The 2030 Plot: Hidden Reasons Why Indigenous Churches Are Suddenly Backing Constitutional Changes
more.description: As Zimbabwe stands at a critical crossroads, a sudden and massive wave of support for Constitutional Amendment No. 3 has emerged from an unexpected quarter: the indigenous churches. While the official narrative suggests a spiritual alignment with national development, our investigation reveals a much deeper, more calculated political manoeuvre. more.title: Woman strangles to death newly-born baby, buries body in shallow grave
more.description: IN an act of sheer cruelty, a 32-year-old woman from Mvurwi in Mashonaland Central delivered a bouncing baby girl, strangled her to death before shoving the body in a shallow grave. Police confirmed the incident which led to the arrest of Thandiwe Kangara last week. “The ZRP confirms the arrest of Thandiwe Kangara (32) in [...]The post Woman strangles to death newly-born baby, buries body in shallow grave appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com.
